name: Consignee confirmation (add to notification)
pageTitle: Create consignee confirmation - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP
heading: The consignee has been created

structure.govukComponent: NOT a GDS caption. Legacy IPAFFS markup: div.govuk-!-padding-top-6 > span.heading-tertiary containing span#reference-number (DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979) + span ( - CHEDP). Sits in the width-container above the grid row, outside <main>.

govukComponents.usage: Confirmation panel containing the H1 'The consignee has been created' (govuk-panel__title). No body text inside the panel.
govukComponents.evidence:
trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 110 (main.html:

The consignee has been created

)

govukComponents.usage: Primary submit button 'Add to notification' (id=button-add, type=submit, value='Add to notification', data-prevent-double-click=true); POSTs the form and navigates to the traders page (.../notifications/{chedReference}/traders). This is the ONLY control the QA tests drive on this page — the page object exposes a single member, btnAddToNotification (getByRole('button', {name:'Add to notification'})).
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 110 (button HTML + navigation to .../DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/traders); corroborated by page-objects/notification/ConsigneeConfirmationPage.ts:6-8 and workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:962

govukComponents.usage: Secondary action 'Return to search' (id=manage-notifications) with href /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/traders/consignee/search. Trace-confirmed as rendered, but NEVER exercised by the QA tests — the ConsigneeConfirmationPage page object omits it entirely, so its behaviour is unasserted.
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 110 (main.html: Return to search); absence from page-objects/notification/ConsigneeConfirmationPage.ts confirms it is not test-driven

nonStandardPatterns.description: The CHED reference + type ('DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP') is rendered NOT via the GDS govuk-caption-* component but via legacy IPAFFS markup: div.govuk-!-padding-top-6 > span.heading-tertiary > span#reference-number + span. It sits in div.govuk-width-container above the grid row, outside <main>. Rebuilt IUU app should render this as a standard GDS section caption (govuk-caption-l/xl) inside the page, not a bespoke heading-tertiary span.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 110 (ancestor chain: SPAN.heading-tertiary < DIV.govuk-!-padding-top-6 < DIV.govuk-width-container app-width-container--wide < BODY)

sourceCorroboration.claim: This page has zero data-entry / input fields — it is a pure confirmation interstitial. The only non-visible inputs are hidden crumb (CSRF) and etag (optimistic-concurrency) fields inside the POST form.
sourceCorroboration.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 110 (main.html form contains only input[name=crumb][hidden], input[name=etag][hidden], the submit button and the Return-to-search link); corroborated by page-objects/notification/ConsigneeConfirmationPage.ts:6-8 which exposes exactly one member (btnAddToNotification) and no input/textbox/dropdown locators.

sourceCorroboration.claim: The page is a distinct, named step in the notification journey, reached after Consignee Creation (Save and continue) and before Traders Addresses.
sourceCorroboration.evidence: workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:406-415 (creation -> btnSaveAndContinue -> consigneeConfirmation.btnAddToNotification -> tradersAddresses); same sequence at ched-p-workflows.ts:962. Trace-confirmed: clicking 'Add to notification' at action 110 navigates to .../DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/traders.

sourceCorroboration.claim: The confirmation page is required to pass automated accessibility (axe/WCAG) checks.
sourceCorroboration.evidence: tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:175 clicks pages.consigneeConfirmation.btnAddToNotification within an accessibility flow; same assertion in tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:136.

sourceCorroboration.claim: The 'Add to notification' button is a data-prevent-double-click primary submit — no test asserts the panel copy, so 'The consignee has been created' is trace-observed only.
sourceCorroboration.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 110 (button carries data-prevent-double-click=true; H1 string read directly from main.html). No page object or test asserts the heading string 'The consignee has been created', 'Add to notification' or 'Return to search' — ConsigneeConfirmationPage.ts locates the button by role/name only.

sourceCorroboration.claim: This confirmation interstitial only appears on the CREATE-NEW-CONSIGNEE branch (search existing -> 'Create a new consignee' -> Consignee Creation -> Save and continue -> THIS page, fromCreate=true). Selecting a pre-existing consignee from search would bypass creation, and hence this confirmation, entirely.
sourceCorroboration.evidence: URL carries ?fromCreate=true (trace back-link href .../consignee/confirmation/{consigneeId}?fromCreate=true). Every workflow reaches this page via searchExistingConsignee.linkCreateNewConsignee -> consigneeCreation.btnSaveAndContinue -> consigneeConfirmation.btnAddToNotification (ched-p-workflows.ts around lines 404-411, 962). The select-an-existing-consignee path is never modelled or driven by the QA suite, so the branch that skips this page is unasserted.

openQuestions: This is an interstitial confirmation/success page shown after creating a new consignee (fromCreate=true), not a data-entry page — it has no user-input fields, only two actions (Add to notification / Return to search) plus a JS 'Back' link. Corroborated: the QA page object (ConsigneeConfirmationPage.ts) exposes only btnAddToNotification. In the rebuilt IUU app this success-then-add step may be unnecessary if consignee selection is inlined into the trader/consignee flow.

The 'Return to search' secondary link is trace-confirmed as rendered (href /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/traders/consignee/search) but is never exercised by any QA test (the page object omits it). Its behaviour is therefore unasserted by the automation — flagged for a human to confirm whether the rebuilt app needs a 'return without adding' escape hatch here.

RESOLVED (was: caption wrapper unverified). The caption 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP' is rendered by legacy IPAFFS markup, not a GDS caption: div.govuk-!-padding-top-6 > span.heading-tertiary > span#reference-number + span, sitting in div.govuk-width-container above the grid row, outside <main>. See nonStandardPatterns. Rebuilt IUU app should use a standard govuk-caption-* inside the page.
The form carries hidden crumb (CSRF) and etag (optimistic-concurrency) inputs — infrastructure, not user requirements.

The confirmation copy is CHED-P specific in provenance ('The consignee has been created'); wording is generic to consignee creation and not IUU-specific. No test asserts this string, so it is trace-observed only and could change without breaking the suite.

This page corroborates from the CHED-P workflow because IPAFFS files IUU (fishery products) as CHED-P; the identical consignee-confirmation step also appears in the CHED-D accessibility flow (ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:136), confirming it is a shared economic-operator sub-journey rather than a CHED-P-specific page.

This confirmation page is reached only when a NEW consignee is created; the QA suite never exercises the 'select an existing consignee from search results' branch, so we have no trace or test evidence for what a returning user selecting an existing consignee sees. For the rebuilt IUU app, a human should confirm whether the create-vs-reuse split (and this success interstitial) is worth preserving or should collapse into an inline consignee selection.
